title: persona update builds SQL via string concatenation of conditionals
description: In persona.rs update() (lines 100-128), the UPDATE query is built by conditionally pushing SQL fragments into a Vec then joining them, with bind parameters added in matching conditional order. This requires the if-let chain (lines 116-127) to exactly mirror the push order (lines 101-112) — a maintenance hazard. If a future change reorders one but not the other, binds will silently attach to wrong columns. A query builder or a fixed UPDATE with COALESCE (e.g. 'SET display_name = COALESCE(?, display_name)') would decouple the two.
close_reason: The dynamic SQL in persona::update is 4 possible fields. The Vec<clause>/Vec<value> pattern is standard and readable. COALESCE would require passing all fields every time including unchanged ones.
